heading: Dealers in securities
label: 1236 Dealers in securities

children.text: the security was, before the close of the day on which it was acquired (or such earlier time as the Secretary may prescribe by regulations), clearly identified in the dealer’s records as a security held for investment; and
the security was not, at any time after the close of such day (or such earlier time), held by such dealer primarily for sale to customers in the ordinary course of his trade or business.

children.heading: Ordinary losses
children.label: b Ordinary losses
children.text: Loss by a dealer in securities from the sale or exchange of any security shall, except as otherwise provided in section 582(c), (relating to bond, etc., losses of banks), in no event be considered as ordinary loss if at any time the security was clearly identified in the dealer’s records as a security held for investment.

children.heading: Definition of security
children.label: c Definition of security
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “security” means any share of stock in any corporation, certificate of stock or interest in any corporation, note, bond, debenture, or evidence of indebtedness, or any evidence of an interest in or right to subscribe to or purchase any of the foregoing.

children.heading: Special rule for floor specialists
children.label: d Special rule for floor specialists
A
children.text: by inserting “the 7th business day following” before “the day” the first place it appears in paragraph (1) and by inserting “7th business” before “day” in paragraph (2), and

children.label: B
children.text: by striking the parenthetical phrase in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: a member of a national securities exchange,

children.label: B
children.text: is registered as a specialist with the exchange, and

children.label: C
children.text: meets the requirements for specialists established by the Securities and Exchange Commission.

children.heading: Special rule for options
children.label: e Special rule for options
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), any security acquired by a dealer pursuant to an option held by such dealer may be treated as held for investment only if the dealer, before the close of the day on which the option was acquired, clearly identified the option on his records as held for investment. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “option” includes the right to subscribe to or purchase any security.
